title: Thin Scope, Vertical Slices, and Evidence-Driven Roadmap · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for thin scope, vertical slices, and evidence-driven roadmap contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.
Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Story Outcome
steps.instruction:
debug case — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

Inspect the Story Outcome trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Story Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Story Outcome, keep Story Outcome connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Story Outcome, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Story Outcome, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Story Outcome precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Story Outcome, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Story Outcome, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A component or database task is automatically a user story.

stimulus.title: Story Outcome case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A component or database task is automatically a user story.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. Artifact under review: risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Story Outcome, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Scope Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Scope Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Scope Tradeoff.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Scope Tradeoff, keep Scope Tradeoff connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.
